originalTitle: Chad warns Sudan it will retaliate after drone strike on mourners kills 17
title: Chad Takes Steps to Keep Everyone Safe
content:
Hello, Friends from Africa!

Hello, my amazing friends! I'm your friendly news reporter for Africa, here to share some important news in a way that's easy for everyone to understand. Sometimes, the news can be a little bit serious, but it's always good to know what's happening around us. Today, we're going to talk about two countries that are neighbors, just like you might have a neighbor next door. These countries are called Chad and Sudan. They are both in our wonderful continent of Africa!

What Happened Between Chad and Sudan?

Imagine two big houses next to each other. Sometimes, things can get a bit noisy or tricky between neighbors. Recently, something sad happened near the border of Chad and Sudan. The border is like an invisible line that tells us where one country ends and another begins. People were gathered together in a place called Sudan. They were very sad because someone they cared about had passed away. When people are sad together like this, we call them "mourners." A flying robot, which we call a "drone," came and caused a big problem. This drone hurt some of the people who were sad. Sadly, 17 people lost their lives. This made everyone in Chad very, very sad and worried. It's like if something bad happened in your neighbor's yard, and it hurt people right next to your house.

Chad's President Makes Important Decisions

When something like this happens, the leaders of countries need to make big decisions to keep everyone safe. The leader of Chad is called President Mahamat Déby. He is like the head of a very big family, which is the country of Chad. President Déby heard about what happened and felt very strongly that he needed to protect his people. He wants to make sure that nothing bad like this happens again to the people of Chad. So, President Déby made some important announcements. First, he told all the soldiers in Chad to be extra, extra ready. We call this "high alert." It means they need to be very watchful and prepared for anything. Imagine if your parents told you to be extra careful when playing outside because there might be a big storm coming. That's a bit like being on high alert.

Closing the Border

The second big thing President Déby did was to close the border between Chad and Sudan. Remember that invisible line we talked about? Well, now, no one is allowed to cross it. It's like when your mom closes the gate to your garden so that no one can get in or out for a little while, to keep everyone safe inside. This is called a "total closure." This means that people cannot travel from Chad into Sudan, and people cannot travel from Sudan into Chad. It’s a way to keep things calm and to make sure everyone in Chad stays safe while they figure out what happened and what to do next. It’s a serious step, but it’s done to protect people.

What is a Drone?

You might be wondering, "What exactly is a drone?" A drone is like a small, flying robot that can be controlled by someone far away. Some drones are used for fun, like taking pictures from the sky or racing. But some drones are bigger and are used by armies. These big drones can sometimes carry things that can cause harm, which is what happened near the border. Did you know? * Drones can fly high in the sky, sometimes without anyone knowing they are there! * Many people use small drones to deliver packages, like a flying postman! * Scientists use drones to study animals and plants in hard-to-reach places.

Why Did This Happen?

This sad event happened because there has been some fighting in Sudan, Chad's neighbor. When there is fighting in one place, sometimes it can make things difficult for the places nearby. It’s like when your friend next door is having a big argument, and it makes everyone in your house feel a bit tense too. Chad wants to make sure that the problems in Sudan don't cause harm to its own people. It’s very important for countries to be good neighbors and to try to solve problems peacefully. When things are peaceful, everyone can be happy and safe. The fighting in Sudan has caused many people to leave their homes and look for safety, sometimes in countries like Chad.

Being Good Neighbors

Just like in school or at home, it's always best when everyone gets along. Countries are the same! When countries are good neighbors, they help each other, share things, and don't cause trouble. When things get difficult, like they have between Chad and Sudan, it's important to talk and try to find a way to make things better. The leaders of Chad want to make sure their country is strong and safe. They are speaking up to say that what happened was not okay and that they want to protect their people. This is how countries sometimes try to solve big problems. Did you know? * Africa has 54 countries! That's a lot of neighbors! * Many countries work together in groups to help each other and keep the peace. * Good neighbors are important for a happy community, whether it's two people or two countries.

Hopes for Peace and Safety

Even though this news is a bit sad, it's important to remember that many people are working hard to make sure everyone in Chad and Sudan can live in peace and be safe. Leaders and other helpers are always trying to find ways to stop fighting and help people live together happily. We hope that very soon, the people in Chad will feel safe again, and that the sad fighting in Sudan will stop. Everyone in Africa wants a peaceful and happy home for all its children and families. Let's all hope for kindness and peace between Chad and Sudan, and all our wonderful African neighbors.
summary: Chad's President is making sure his country stays safe after a drone caused harm near the border with Sudan, ordering soldiers to be ready and closing the border.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows how countries protect their people and try to keep everyone safe when there is trouble nearby, helping to keep peace in our big African family.

originalTitle: Denounce 'abject' Afcon decision - senior Caf member
title: Uh Oh! Senegal Won't Host the Big Football Party Anymore!
content:
Hello, Football Fans!

Hello, my super awesome football friends across Africa! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, ready to share some important news about our favourite game, football! We all love football, right? The cheering, the goals, the excitement! Today, we have some news about a very special football party called AFCON. Get ready to listen carefully, because it's a bit of a tricky story, but we'll make it simple for everyone to understand.

What is AFCON? Our Big African Football Party!

AFCON stands for the Africa Cup of Nations. Wow, that's a big name! But what does it mean? It's like the biggest, most exciting football tournament just for countries in Africa! Imagine all the best teams from different African countries coming together to play. They play to see who is the champion of Africa! It happens every two years, and it's a huge celebration. Everyone gets together to watch their favourite teams. It's full of fun, music, and amazing football skills! Did you know? The Africa Cup of Nations has been happening for a very long time! The first one was way back in 1957. That's even before your parents were born!

Being the Host: A Special Job!

Now, every time we have this big party, one country gets to be the 'host'. What does 'host' mean? It means they are the ones who get to have the party in their house, or in this case, in their country! They build beautiful stadiums, make sure everything is ready, and welcome all the other teams and fans. It's a very special job, and it makes the host country feel very proud! It's like being chosen to host the best sleepover ever! Did you know? Hosting a big event like AFCON helps the country a lot! They build new roads, new hotels, and new stadiums. It also gives jobs to many people in the country!

Sad News for Senegal: No More Hosting 2025 AFCON

Now for the news that might make some of us feel a little bit sad. Our friends in Senegal were chosen to be the hosts for the AFCON party in the year 2025. Isn't that wonderful? They were so excited to welcome everyone! But, recently, a big group called CAF made a new decision. CAF is like the boss of football in Africa. They decided that Senegal will not host the 2025 AFCON anymore. Yes, that's right. Senegal will not be the host. This news might make many people in Senegal and across Africa feel a bit disappointed. It's like planning a big birthday party, and then suddenly, you find out you can't have it at your house anymore. It feels a bit unfair, doesn't it?

Who is CAF? The Football Bosses of Africa

Let's talk a bit more about CAF. CAF stands for the Confederation of African Football. They are the important people who make rules and decisions about football for all of Africa. They help organize all the big tournaments, like AFCON, and make sure everything runs smoothly. Some important people work in CAF. One of them is a man named Augustin Senghor. He is a very important member of the CAF team. He heard the news about Senegal not hosting and he said it was 'unacceptable' and 'abject'. These are big words, but they mean he thinks the decision is really, really not good and wrong. He believes that it's not fair to take away the hosting job from Senegal. He feels very strongly about it, just like you would feel if something you were looking forward to was suddenly changed.

Why Did This Decision Happen?

You might be wondering, 'Why did CAF change their mind?' Well, sometimes, when a country is getting ready to host a very big party like AFCON, there are many, many things to do. You need perfect stadiums, good places for the teams to stay, and safe ways for everyone to travel. Sometimes, countries need more time to get everything super ready. Maybe CAF thought Senegal needed more time to prepare everything perfectly for such a huge event. They want to make sure the AFCON party is the very best it can be for everyone. Did you know? Preparing to host a big football tournament can take many years! Countries need to plan very carefully and build many new things.

What Happens Next for AFCON 2025?

So, if Senegal won't host, who will? Well, CAF will now look for another country in Africa to host the 2025 AFCON. There are many other wonderful African countries that would love to host this amazing party! We will find out soon who the new host will be. Even though Senegal won't be the host, their amazing football team can still play in the tournament! Senegal has a very strong football team. They are actually the current champions of AFCON! They won the last AFCON in 2021. So, we will still see their fantastic players on the field, trying to win again! Did you know? Senegal's football team is nicknamed the 'Lions of Teranga'! Teranga means 'hospitality' in their language, Wolof. They are known for being very welcoming!

Fair Play and Our Love for African Football!

In football, we always talk about fair play. It means playing by the rules and being fair to everyone. And this idea of fair play is important even when we talk about hosting big events. Mr. Augustin Senghor wants everyone to remember what is fair. It's a tricky situation, but we know that African football is very strong and full of passionate people. No matter where the AFCON 2025 happens, we know it will be an amazing celebration of football! Let's keep cheering for all our African teams and remember that football brings us all together, no matter what! Keep loving the beautiful game, my friends! Did you know? Football is the most popular sport in Africa! Millions of children and adults love to play and watch it every single day!
summary: Senegal will no longer host the big Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) football party in 2025, a decision that a senior football official called "unacceptable."
whyMatters: This matters because hosting AFCON is a very special and proud job for an African country, bringing excitement and building new things, so changing the host can make many people feel disappointed and question if it's fair.
